title: Translation Tables
text: Translation Tables To mediate between itself and the external environment, Uniface uses translation tables. Translation tables provide input and output mappings between platform-specific device codes and Uniface functions, and between external and internal character sets. Uniface provides translation tables for the platforms it supports, but you can customize these or use them to create your own tables. Translation tables are compiled sets of definitions that map platform-specific input and output codes to Uniface internal meta codes. All interaction with input and output devices is table-driven. This allows Uniface to work with a wide variety of devices without knowing the details for each one. There are four types of translation table, which are classified as either keyboard translation tables, which handle input data streams from keyboards and databases, or as device translation tables, which handle output data streams to screens and printers. Table 1. Types of Translation Table Type Description device translation tables Map Uniface logical functions and meta characters to output device functions Display Map Uniface logical functions and meta characters to device display code Printer Map Uniface logical functions and meta characters to printer code keyboard translation tables Map input codes to Uniface logical function codes and meta characters Keyboard Map keyboard codes to Uniface logical function codes and meta characters DBMS Map meta code to meta code. DBMS translation tables provide database translation capability for fields in form components. For every system supported, Uniface supplies a set of translation tables in the Dynamic Object Library usys.uar . These should be sufficient in most cases. If the translation table you require is not present, you can define your own. It is usually much easier to copy an existing table and modify it, than to define an entirely new one yourself. The translation table definitions belong to the USYS library and are available in libdvc.usys.xml and libktt.usys.xml . Related Links Meta Character Sets Meta character sets are used for translating installable character sets and defining translation tables. In earlier versions of Uniface, meta character sets were used as internal character sets instead of Unicode. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
